sections.paragraph:
m. Isa 5:2 Jer 2:21. שׂרֵקָה f. Gen 49:11

(1) a shoot, a tendril (from the root שָׂרַק No. I; compare שְׂרוּקִּים); specially a nobler kind of vine, according to Abulwalid, growing in Syria, Arab. سريق and سوريق, now called in Morocco Serki, Pers. ىشمش Kishmish, with small berries, roundish, and of a dark colour; with soft and hardly perceptible stones. See Niebuhr’s Reisebeschr. vol. ii. p. 169; of his Descr. of Arabia, p. 147; and more in Oedmann, Verm. Sammlungen aus der Naturkunde, vi. p. 98, seqq.

(2) [Sorek], pr.n. of a valley, between Ashkelon and Gaza, probably so called from this kind of vine, Jud. 16:4 .
